reason: SearchResult became a behavior-bearing frozen dataclass (quarry-gcnf): the result_similarity free function was deleted and method_ratio restored to 1.0. avg_params (0->0.86) and classes_per_module (1->2) rose as the natural shape of adding a proper domain class with methods, both well within absolute thresholds (4.0 / 3). avg_complexity improved 3.0->1.29. Recording the genuine improvement, not laundering a regression.

Added __post_init__ distance-range invariant guard (quarry-gcnf): avg_params improved 0.86->0.75 (the guard is a zero-param method), avg_complexity ticked up 1.29->1.38 as the natural cost of one validation branch, still far below the 10 threshold. method_ratio holds at 1.0; module_size 173 well under 300. Recording a net hardening improvement, not laundering a regression.

reject_if_running extracted to RouteGroup as the shared singleton guard for sync+optimize+backfill (Copilot: optimize/backfill were non-singleton; a concurrent backfill double-ingests sessions). DRYs the 3rd copy of the 409 check; sync.py improved -9 module_size in the same change.

optimize + backfill each gained a reject_if_running singleton guard + correctness-rationale docstring (Copilot: concurrent optimize contends for the table lock, concurrent backfill double-ingests); module_size 118->131, avg_complexity 3.67->4.0. New vs merge-base and clears all absolute thresholds.

require_json_content_type added to shared RequestGuards — CSRF guard rejecting non-JSON on optimize/backfill with 415; module_size 73->89. avg_params 2.67->2.25 and avg_complexity 4.0->3.5 improved in the same change (guard is small + branch-light).

SECURITY: scrub_and_log applied to transcript text before ingest_content, restoring the DES-036 scrub-at-write invariant — raw transcript secrets/PII were reaching LanceDB and were retrievable via /v1/search. Adds the scrub import, the scrub call, and a security-rationale comment.

SECURITY: content-type CSRF guard on optimize+backfill + remote limit<=0 clamp (_bounded_limit + DEFAULT_REMOTE_BACKFILL_LIMIT) so an empty remote backfill cannot run unbounded; module_size 131->158, avg_complexity up for the added guard branches. New vs merge-base; clears all absolute thresholds.

SECURITY: JsonContentTypeGuard ASGI middleware added — the fail-closed CSRF choke point enforcing application/json on every POST (djb ruling), wired next to CORS in _add_middleware. Adds a 2nd app-assembly class (classes_per_module 1->2) + module_size/complexity for __new__/__call__.

SECURITY: RemoteClient always advertises Content-Type: application/json (even for a bodyless request) so an argument-free POST clears the daemon's new 415 CSRF guard; +rationale comment. module_size 267->276.

OptimizeOutcome result object added — optimize() now reports whether it actually ran vs skipped (Copilot: /optimize claimed success on a threshold/no-table skip and double-counted fragments). +class (classes_per_module 1->2) + module_size for the outcome + reason building; fragments now counted once.

BackfillRequest.limit docstring corrected to describe the remote clamp (1..500) instead of the stale 'limit==0 means no limit' (Copilot: it's surfaced in OpenAPI and misled remote callers). +doc lines.

openapi_extra now emits an application/json requestBody for model-less POSTs too (Copilot: sync/captures/use advertised no body, mismatching the content-type guard); +branch (complexity) + module_size.
_run_optimize reports the actual OptimizeOutcome (optimized/reason) instead of hardcoded success (Copilot finding 1+2); +result-dict + reason branch. New vs merge-base, clears absolute thresholds.

response_model widened to type[BaseModel] | UnionType and /show set to ShowPageResponse | DocumentInfo so its dual 200 shape (page text vs metadata) is documented in OpenAPI (Copilot); +DocumentInfo/UnionType imports + rationale comments.

security fix (cursor): add non-raising loopback_token/loopback_token_for_url probes so login + remote --ping present the live serve.token; new client-tier file, passes absolute thresholds
security fix (cursor MEDIUM): try/finally removes serve.token on any startup/bind failure; module_size 148->158 still improves vs merge-base (165)
security hardening (rmh LOW): empty/corrupt serve.token fails closed (never an empty bearer) + non-raising loopback probes; new client-tier file, all absolute thresholds pass

security fix (cursor HIGH): write serve.token AFTER a successful bind + guard removal by _bound so a failed second quarryd cannot clobber a running peer's token; the startup-hook guard logic adds max_complexity 3->4 / avg_complexity +0.27; module_size -26 and 4 other metrics improve vs merge-base
security fix (cursor MEDIUM): case-insensitive localhost match (RFC 4343) — lowercase host + rationale comment; +4 lines, still well under thresholds

security fix (cursor MEDIUM #1): process-scoped active_db so the client tier resolves the daemon's --db startup-db run dir for serve.token; +13 lines (ClassVar + set_active_db/active_db), avg_complexity improved 3.5->2.83, still far under module_size threshold

security fix (cursor #1 + #3): resolve serve.token from the active-db run dir + reword the fail-closed message to not assert a single cause (daemon-down OR unreadable token); new client-tier file, all absolute thresholds pass

security fix (cursor #1): CLI main callback records the --db active database so the client tier resolves the daemon's serve.token run dir; +4 lines; module_size still -52 vs merge-base, 4 metrics improved

security fix (copilot): gate remote --ping fallback on loopback-vs-remote so a loopback target uses the LIVE serve.token only (never a stored bearer); +6 lines; module_size still -48 vs merge-base, 4 metrics improved
security fix (copilot): add is_loopback_url so a caller distinguishes loopback (live-token-only) from remote (stored bearer); new client-tier file, all absolute thresholds pass

security fix (cursor): all-or-nothing sidecar pair — try/except removes both on a failed write so startup never leaves a partial (lone port / orphan token); the guard adds max_complexity 3->5 / avg_complexity +0.49 on the startup hook; module_size -16 and 5 metrics improved
security fix (cursor): use LoopbackPolicy for the install-time host classification (one source of truth for loopback, matching the daemon); +1 line, far under threshold

security fix (copilot): _login_bearer treats an empty/whitespace bearer as absent (returns None) so remote_mapping emits no empty Authorization header — mirrors the loopback empty-token fail-closed on the stored-header path; new client-tier file, all absolute thresholds pass

security fix (cursor BLOCKING): airtight clobber guard — _write_sidecars extracted, removes ONLY sidecars this instance wrote (wrote_token/wrote_port flags) + atomic PortFile; the flag logic adds avg_complexity to ~2.0 (down from 2.56 pre-extraction); extraction improved max_complexity 7->5; module_size and 5 metrics improved vs merge-base
security fix (cursor MEDIUM): login uses the live serve.token for loopback (never a stale env key); +is_loopback_host gate; module_size still improved vs merge-base
security fix (cursor): add is_loopback_host (host-based loopback check for the login path) + empty-bearer guard; new client-tier file, all absolute thresholds pass

security fix (cursor BLOCKING): PortFile.write made atomic (tmp+rename+cleanup) so a failed write leaves no partial — required for the wrote_port-flag clobber guard; new file, all absolute thresholds pass

security fix (cursor HIGH): distinct per-sidecar temp names (serve.port.tmp / serve.token.tmp) + O_EXCL 0600-from-creation _create_0600 helper so the token is never written into a stale, world-readable temp; new file, all absolute thresholds pass (module_size 160/300, avg_complexity 1.43/5.0)

security fix (findings 2+3, MEDIUM): _authenticated normalizes the api_key once at the daemon boundary (strip → serve.token/auth/client agree) and refuses a keyless bind (R4: loopback ⇒ token-required, no silent open); real R4-enforcement method + rationale docstring; absolute thresholds pass, also dropped the LoopbackPolicy import (efferent coupling ↓)

security fix (finding 4, LOW): _login_bearer parses scheme/token and compares the scheme case-insensitively (matches the daemon's parts[0].lower()=='bearer'), so a stored 'bearer <tok>' resolves instead of being dropped client-side; new client-tier file, all absolute thresholds pass

security fix (finding 5, LOW): LoopbackPolicy normalizes strip()+one trailing dot before the name match so 'localhost.'/' localhost ' is not misclassified remote and wrongly key-gated; new file, all absolute thresholds pass

HIGH (token-exfiltration fix): split LoopbackPolicy into is_loopback (name-tolerant BIND gate) vs is_literal_loopback (literal-IP-only TOKEN-PRESENTATION gate) + canonical_host (localhost→127.0.0.1) + extracted _normalized; a NAME must never trigger presentation of the secret serve.token; new file, absolute thresholds pass

HIGH (token-exfiltration fix): present the live serve.token only to a literal loopback IP (is_literal_loopback), never a name; + canonical_host staticmethod for login-time localhost→127.0.0.1 canonicalization; new client-tier file, absolute thresholds pass

HIGH (token-exfiltration fix): login_cmd canonicalizes a loopback NAME to the IPv4 literal (ClientConfig.canonical_host) before storing/validating, so the managed path pins 127.0.0.1 and never stores the ambiguous localhost; +5 lines + rationale comment on the CLI god module, absolute thresholds pass

security fix (finding 1, MEDIUM): a loopback login persists NO bearer (R4 live-only) — stored_key omits the api_key for a literal-loopback target so a stale QUARRY_API_KEY is never saved; +rationale comment on the CLI god module, absolute thresholds pass

security fix (finding 2, MEDIUM): normalize the api_key ONCE at the launcher boundary (_normalized: strip + empty→None) so enforce_bind_key, _effective_key, and DaemonServer agree — a whitespace key is absent everywhere (loopback mints, network refuses at the gate); new-file absolute thresholds pass
Read-path loopback migration (canonical_url + _target_host) closes the stale-localhost lockout HIGH; +2 methods are irreducible security substance, module_size 261<300
remote --ping canonicalizes the probe URL so the live serve.token is presented only to the literal loopback, never the ambiguous name
canonical_host returns normalized (stripped) host for non-loopback names, closing the invalid-URL from unstripped whitespace LOW; docstring adds the threat rationale
corrected stale module docstring (quarryd entrypoint + both serve.port/serve.token sidecars) grows module_size; removing the dead keyless branch in _write_sidecars improves max/avg complexity
corrected canonical_host docstring to state it normalizes (stripped/lowercased/de-dotted), not byte-for-byte unchanged; documentation substance grows module_size
to_netloc brackets IPv6 literal hosts so host:port URLs parse (login ::1 was broken); a stateless URL primitive beside ws_to_http in a primitives module, avg_complexity improved
login brackets an IPv6 literal host via to_netloc so the stored ws_url parses; import + rationale comment grow module_size

LoopbackPolicy._normalized strips IPv6 URL brackets so [::1] classifies as loopback (closes remote-misclassification + wrong key-gating); the added guard raises avg_complexity, docstring documents the threat
run-dir exclusive flock makes the daemon the sole sidecar writer (closes shared-run-dir clobber + token temp-retry race); _write_sidecars catches BaseException so a SIGINT leaves no lone sidecar
_login_bearer mirrors the daemon's split() bearer contract (exactly 2 parts) so tab-separated and space-containing tokens agree client/server; rationale comments grow module_size
RunDir.lock_path names the daemon's serve.lock advisory-lock file (sole-writer ownership); property + threat docstring grow module_size, absolute thresholds pass

_serve_token moves settings resolution inside the try and catches (OSError, ValueError) so a corrupt default-db config fails closed (loopback_token stays non-raising); rationale comments grow module_size
_acquire_run_dir_lock closes the fd on ALL flock failure paths (adds except BaseException) so a non-contention OSError never leaks a descriptor; sets _lock_fd only after the lock is held
fcntl imported optionally (POSIX-only) so the module imports on non-POSIX; a daemon start there fails closed; _release_run_dir_lock closes the fd in finally so a failing LOCK_UN never leaks
_quarryd_exec_args canonicalizes a loopback-NAME QUARRY_SERVE_HOST to 127.0.0.1 so the daemon bind agrees with the install probe and login target; rationale comment grows module_size

_normalized canonicalizes a loopback-name host to 127.0.0.1 at the single bind point so a DIRECT quarryd --host localhost agrees with the probe/login literal; the dual-path rationale docstring grows module_size, absolute thresholds pass

canonical_url wraps the urlsplit/port parse in try/except ValueError so a malformed stored URL (wss://localhost:bad) fails closed (returns unchanged for the connection layer) instead of crashing the CLI; the guard adds a branch and rationale comment

_target_host now derives the token-gate host from canonical_url (the migrated url) so the gate and the connection can never diverge; a malformed URL fails closed at the gate too (no serve.token read for a resolver-controlled name); rationale docstring grows module_size

wire two daemon-health checks (reachability + serve.token) into the aggregator: +1 import, +2 list entries. The checks live in a NEW cohesive DaemonDiagnostics class (doctor_daemon.py, method_ratio 1.0) rather than inflating this god module with free _check_ functions; god-module decomposition is separate

bracket an IPv6 literal host in the startup listening log via to_netloc (display only, bind unchanged): import + rationale comment grow module_size; renders https://[::1]:8420, not the ambiguous ::1:8420

lock hardening: O_CLOEXEC on the serve.lock fd (no leak into spawned subprocesses) + best-effort release (swallow+log a failing LOCK_UN, never re-raise from run()'s finally so it can't mask the real shutdown error); the except branch and rationale comments grow module_size/avg_complexity

validate_connection 401 message is loopback-aware: a literal-loopback target authenticates with the daemon's serve.token (not --api-key), so its 401 points at serve.token + quarry doctor; the nested is_literal_loopback branch grows max_complexity, the message grows module_size

os.close in _release_run_dir_lock is now best-effort too (its own try/except OSError, swallow+log): release truly never raises from run()'s finally, so a failing close can never mask the real shutdown reason; the added except grows complexity/module_size

_probe_health tries HTTPS then falls back to plain HTTP on a TLS handshake failure, so a plaintext quarryd (no --tls) is reported reachable; the extracted _probe_conn helper grows module_size and avg_params

CaptureUrl delegates IPv6 bracketing to the shared to_netloc primitive and deletes the duplicate _bracketed method; module_size shrinks and duplication is gone, but removing a low-complexity method raises avg_complexity (a metric artifact, the module is simpler)
the run()-start log brackets an IPv6 bind host via to_netloc (display only, bind unchanged); rationale comment grows module_size
_create_0600 adds O_CLOEXEC to the token temp fd (no inherit into a spawned subprocess); rationale comment grows module_size, absolute thresholds pass

login strips the operator --api-key ((api_key or '').strip() or None), mirroring the daemon's DaemonServer._authenticated so a whitespace-suffixed key round-trips instead of 401ing; the two short-circuit 'or's add decision points to login_cmd and the comment grows module_size

_normalized fails closed on an api_key with INTERNAL whitespace (Bearer <key> would split into 3+ parts, permanently 401-ing the daemon), rejecting it at the single normalization seam for all binds; the guard + any() generator and the rationale docstring grow complexity/module_size

documented dismissal of the close-EINTR-retry finding: expand the os.close swallow comment with the close(2)/PEP 475 rationale (fd released on EINTR on Linux/macOS; retrying close is the reused-fd anti-pattern; LOCK_UN + process-exit free the flock regardless); a security/portability rationale comment, not trimmable to move module_size

PortFile.write cleans up its temp on BaseException (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit too, not just OSError) so an interrupt never leaves a stale serve.port.tmp, mirroring ServeTokenFile.write; rationale comment grows module_size, absolute thresholds pass

launcher now enforces TLS on a non-loopback bind (enforce_bind_tls) alongside the key gate — irreducible security wiring
cleartext (http/ws) non-loopback env target now refused regardless of token — content, not just the bearer, must not cross the wire in cleartext; irreducible security-gate growth
from_login now refuses a stored cleartext (http/ws) non-loopback login (TLS-or-refused) — irreducible security gate on the stored-login path

add CapturesCollection.fallback() so the fallback collection name lives with the capture-collection logic (relocated from hooks.WEB_CAPTURES_FALLBACK); passes all absolute thresholds as a new file vs merge-base
orphan check now spares the live default-captures fallback (was the stale web-captures); +1 line for the corrected security rationale comment
capture/ingest_url gain an optional timeout so the hook can bound the send (compaction must not wait 15s on a saturated daemon) — irreducible wire-method param

hook capture/ingest send now passes a 5s timeout (constant + rationale) so a saturated daemon never blocks compaction; +8 lines vs the commit-C in-tree baseline, still improves module_size 674->603 vs merge-base

empty-extraction guard: an extraction that yields zero pages must not delete the prior good capture (silent data loss); +8 lines vs the intermediate in-tree baseline, module_size still improves 1280->951 vs merge-base

per-caller unreachable_log (truthful down-daemon message: compaction archives, web-fetch does not) + constants; grows vs the intermediate in-tree baseline but still improves module_size 674->622 and avg_params/avg_complexity vs merge-base
removed ImageExtractor's dead instance API (never constructed); analyze() is now the sole real method, whose inherent complexity sets the average — module_size 121->61 and avg_params both improve
stdio remember now scrubs before storing (closes the last cleartext leak into memory-<agent>) — +5 lines for the scrubber hook + rationale, no offsettable metric
corrected the captures-exclusion comment to state dot-dir pruning is the primary mechanism (reviewer fix) — data-list module, comment growth only

empty/blank cwd guard (route to default-captures instead of Path('').resolve()=daemon cwd) adds a branch + rationale vs the intermediate in-tree baseline; passes all absolute thresholds as a new file vs merge-base

deleting dead _find_registration + the truthful-log constants net-improve hooks vs merge-base (module 674->613, avg_params 1.36->1.30); avg_complexity drifts up vs the intermediate in-tree only because the removed helper was trivial

same fail-closed delete guard applied to ingest_url (empty extraction / scrub error must not delete the prior capture); +9 lines vs the intermediate in-tree baseline, module_size still improves 1280->960 vs merge-base

is_already_ingested now recognizes the hook's stable session-<id> name (not just session-<id>-<mtime>) so an already-captured session is not re-ingested as a duplicate — +1 condition + rationale docstring, no offsettable metric
IngestRequest.scrub field carries explicit web-fetch capture intent (so an empty cwd never routes a capture into the unscrubbed ingest_auto branch) — schema file, no offsettable metric
_capture_name strips inputs and rejects whitespace-only document_name/session_id (+rationale) — new POST /v1/capture endpoint file, +4 lines
scrub coerce branch on _ingest_job (+1 condition for explicit capture intent); avg_params/max_complexity/module_size/classes all improve massively vs merge-base (172->99, 3->1 classes)

ScrubbedIngestJob now scrubs free-form metadata (name/summary) and IngestJob carries explicit scrub intent; grows vs the intermediate in-tree baseline, passes all absolute thresholds as a new file vs merge-base
resolve() failure on untrusted cwd (embedded NUL / OS-invalid path) falls back to default-captures instead of 500 — boundary catch, grows vs intermediate in-tree baseline

_extract_inline_pages carries the redacted URL as document_path so a web-fetch capture keeps its source location (+rationale); +2 lines vs intermediate in-tree, module still improves 1280->962 vs merge-base
compose CaptureIngestJob: thread source_url + wrap ScrubbedIngestJob so a zero-chunk web-fetch capture re-fetches through the SSRF-checked path instead of silently indexing nothing
web-fetch hook carries source_url so the daemon re-fetches a zero-chunk capture (empty-extraction data-loss fix); +7 lines still nets module_size 620 vs merge-base 674
add CaptureIngestJob (3rd job class) composing inline scrub with SSRF-checked URL re-fetch fallback; NEW file, classes_per_module 3<=3 absolute threshold
add source_url field + justification comment carrying the raw fetch URL for the daemon's zero-chunk re-fetch fallback; NEW file, module_size well under 300

mirror the /ingest SSRF boundary on /v1/capture: gate source_url via UrlSafetyCheck at the route (fail-closed 400) before the zero-chunk re-fetch, plus CaptureIngestJob composition; complexity 5/10 and module 113/300 stay well under absolute thresholds
CaptureIngestJob docstring documents the route-boundary SSRF gate + trust-an-already-validated-URL contract; NEW file, module_size well under 300
web-fetch comment corrected to state the SSRF gate lives at the capture route (+1 line); module_size 621 vs merge-base 674 still nets an improvement

distinguish daemon connection/http/malformed failures (truthful error classification) + extract _precompact_target to cut handle_pre_compact CC 13->10; module_size 647 still improves on merge-base 674, and avg/max complexity both improve

choke-point metadata scrub in ingest_content + ingest_url (redact document_name/summary when a content_scrubber is present); module 980 and avg_complexity 4.37 both still improve on merge-base 1280/4.71
ScrubbedIngestJob docstring documents the choke-point contract (forward raw metadata, pipeline redacts once); NEW file, module_size well under 300

extract _ProjectProcessor to fix the CC-11 _process_project god function (max_complexity 11->8, avg_params improved) and route backfill through the pipeline scrub choke point; module_size 292 (under 300) and classes_per_module 5 grow to carry that real structural improvement

diagnostics/robustness the review required: ClientConfigError classification in _send_to_daemon + (OSError,ValueError) guard on the untrusted transcript_path resolve; avg_complexity 5.10 (+0.01) is offset in-file by improved avg_params/max_complexity/module_size (658 vs merge-base 674)

thread overwrite param through _chunk_embed_store + _ingest_multipage_image for the fail-closed delete-gate; avg_params 8.11 still improves on merge-base 8.12, and module_size 973 / avg_complexity 4.05 improve substantially (consolidated 5 delete blocks into one choke point)

document_name() docstring records the dedup-unification invariant (stable session-<prefix> matching the hook, no mtime) so a future change can't re-add the suffix and reintroduce two-docs-per-session; +5 lines in a pure value module with no offsetting principal
DES-042: three required ingest-queue settings (embed concurrency, queue depth, drain timeout) grow module_size
DES-042: resident IngestQueue field + accessor + shutdown-drain method grow module_size; same change improves avg_params and avg_complexity
DES-042: RouteGroup.submit (202/503 enqueue seam) grows avg_params and module_size; avg_complexity improves
DES-042: hoisted collection resolution (_ingest_collection) so the queue can key on it; avg_params/module_size carry the method while avg_complexity improves
DES-042: ASGI lifespan drains the ingest queue on shutdown; module_size carries the drain call + ctx access
DES-042: TaskRegistry.drop (rejected-submit cleanup) + terminal-status GC set for the new queued state; method_ratio/avg_complexity improve
DES-042 hazard fixes add three queue-hardening settings (job_timeout, max_workers, worker_idle); module_size growth is the settings fields themselves

Split Module: CollectionWorker extracted to its own module (module_size 199->182, classes 3->1); the denser coordinator raises avg_params/avg_complexity on the smaller class, both well under threshold
drain-abort terminal guard (#7): a job cancelled while awaiting the embed gate now records failed; adds one branch to _run (module_size 125->133, avg_complexity 1.78, both well under threshold)

quarry-3xat/qwpk: total wall-clock deadline + response-size cap thread a deadline through two read helpers (avg_params) and add the bounded-read body (module_size); avg_complexity improves via the iter()-based read loop

quarry-ickn: route now resolves the plain-ingest collection to the actual table (URL host) via the shared IngestCollection resolver before keying the queue; the routing comment + resolve call grow module_size
quarry-eq52: shutdown()/abort() return their task and document why a reaped worker's task must be retained (asyncio holds only a weak ref); docstring growth

quarry-eq52: retain cancelled worker tasks until cancellation completes (_reaped set + _retain helper) so reap/evict/cancel_workers no longer strand a pending task; avg_complexity improves in the same change
quarry-atsz: abort() now spools aborted un-durable jobs via _fail_aborted (extracted method) + JobSpool import; the spool wiring and its rationale grow the module and add one 2-param helper
quarry-atsz: spool_record on all three jobs + a shared _scrubbed helper; avg_params and avg_complexity improve, module_size grows to carry the recovery snapshots
quarry-atsz: IngestUnit gains the spool_record protocol method + its contract docstring; avg_params improves
djb review: create-restricted spool (dir 0o700, file 0o600 via os.open O_EXCL) + Class-1 fd-close/temp-cleanup grow the module and its avg/max complexity
djb review: in-flight job spooled in _run's CancelledError handler (_mark_aborted) + _spool helper close the silent-loss gap; avg_complexity improves, module_size/avg_params grow
Copilot doc-precision: class docstring reworded to match _read_body (deadline checked between reads, worst case deadline + one socket timeout); +2 docstring lines
Copilot review: _abort_reason routes truthful terminal wording through one helper (spool write success vs failure); avg_complexity improves, module_size/avg_params grow for the spool wiring
Copilot review: write() returns bool so a failed spool write is not falsely reported recoverable; +return path grows module_size
Copilot review: fetch wraps deadline TimeoutError with the URL for log debuggability; _reject_non_html extraction improves avg_complexity, module_size/avg_params grow
DES-031 PR-4: mcp_server rewritten procedural→OO — the McpTools class is the decomposition (0→1 class); every other metric improved (method_ratio +0.9, module_size -81, max_complexity 8→3)

DES-031 PR-4: mcp_server procedural→OO — McpTools class is the decomposition (0→1 class); every other metric improved. show()'s 404→friendly-message try/except adds minor complexity, still improved vs base.

DES-031 PR-4 nit#1 (djb): _configure_claude_code now removes any stale quarry entry before add (replace-don't-shadow); +5 lines module_size vs the intra-PR baseline, still improved vs merge-base (944→931); avg_complexity also improved.

DES-031 PR-4: mcp_server procedural→OO — McpTools class is the decomposition (0→1 class, the sole loosened metric); every other metric improved vs merge-base (method_ratio 0→0.895, module_size 445→377, max_complexity 8→6).

DES-031 PR-4: mcp_server procedural→OO — McpTools class is the decomposition (0→1 class, sole loosened metric); every other metric improved vs merge-base. use_database now selects the literal named db (cursor MEDIUM fix).

DES-031 PR-4 fix#1 (cursor MEDIUM): TargetResolver.selects_local_db() predicate lets MCP use() detect a remote/explicit target and refuse a false local switch; +13 module_size (227, well under 300), avg_params and avg_complexity improved.

DES-031 PR-4 fix#3 (cursor MEDIUM): _configure_claude_code add-first replace (remove only when the add reports the entry exists; loud failure if re-add fails after a removal) — never a silent MCP-config wipe; module_size still improved vs merge-base (944→941), avg_complexity improved.

DES-031 PR-4: mcp_server procedural→OO — McpTools class is the decomposition (0→1 class, sole loosened metric); every other metric improved vs merge-base (method_ratio 0→0.895, module_size 445→395, max_complexity 8→6). use() remote-target guard + tool-name doc fixes.

DES-031 PR-4 fix#3: _run_claude helper collapses three subprocess S603 sites into one (fewer suppressions) for the safe add-first replace; avg_params +0.01 (0.72, far under 4.0) is the 2-param helper, offset by avg_complexity 4.42→4.25.

DES-031 PR-4 fix#1 (cursor MEDIUM): selects_local_db now classifies by the winning target's HOST (name-tolerant loopback) so a 'quarry login localhost' install is correctly local, not falsely remote; +avg_params/module_size for the host-check predicate + helper, avg_complexity improved.

DES-031 PR-4 fix#2 (cursor/copilot LOW): _configure_claude_code captures the mcp-remove result and refuses to claim a removal / blind re-add when remove failed (accurate message, no false recovery); +13 module_size for the remove-check branch, avg_complexity improved 4.42→4.28.

DES-031 PR-4: mcp_server procedural→OO — McpTools class is the decomposition (0→1 class, sole loosened metric); every other metric improved vs merge-base (method_ratio 0→0.9, module_size 445→426). Input-validation sweep: _reject_blank guards + page/limit guards.
reject_unsafe adds the SSRF gate for attacker-controlled sitemap entries (+21 lines on a 100-line file, far under the 300 limit); avg_params improves in the same change.
SSRF gate call at the single sitemap fetch choke point (_bulk_ingest_entries); +5 lines on a god module whose full decomposition is tracked separately; no new branch or dependency.
IPv4-mapped IPv6 normalization closes the ::ffff CGNAT bypass; +5 lines and +0.2 avg_complexity for a security-critical address check, both far under limits.
Thread the gated USP web client through both discovery entry points so USP's index/robots/redirect fetches are SSRF-gated; +3 lines of wiring on a 124-line file.

get() now catches the broad transport/body-read error class (IncompleteRead etc.) and fails closed so no exception escapes on the top-level discover path; small growth for a robustness handler, far under limits.

reject_unsafe gains a limit param + early-stop branch so the limit counts SAFE entries (fixes under-delivery) and bounds DNS to ~limit resolutions; avg_params 1.75->1.80, avg_complexity 4.0->4.2, module_size grows, all far under limits.
Reorder filter(glob)->gate(limit) at the sitemap choke point with rationale comments; module_size grows on a god module tracked for decomposition, no new branch or dependency.
build_opener disables env proxies via an empty ProxyHandinaler with security rationale so the fetch goes direct to the gated host; +5 lines, far under the 300 limit.

select_safe fuses glob-filter + SSRF-gate + cap into one lazy pass (plus a _matches helper) so added work scales with limit not sitemap size; include/exclude/limit params raise avg_params to 2.33 and module_size to 151, both far under limits.
Close the intermediate 3xx response fd before aborting a blocked redirect (Class-1 fd-leak fix); small line growth, far under limits.
HTTPError response fd closed to prevent EMFILE, plus 5xx/429 retryable classification; +1 method and a few lines for the fd-leak and retry fixes, far under limits.
Guard fp.close() with contextlib.suppress so a close failure cannot mask the SSRF rejection; small growth, far under limits.
Guard exc.close() with contextlib.suppress so get() honors never-raises even if close fails; small growth, far under limits.
